sanskrit:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान्॥
transliteration:
traiguṇya-viṣayā vedā nistraiguṇyo bhavārjuna |
nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣema ātmavān ||
literal_meaning: The Vedas deal with the three gunas; be beyond the gunas, Arjuna; free from opposites, ever established in sattva, without concern for acquisition or preservation, rooted in the Self.
contextual_meaning: Krishna invites Arjuna to graduate from rule-based security (Vedic ritual) to ego-transcendent inner freedom, shifting identity from anxious doer to silent witness.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: clinging to familiar scripts while sensing they no longer guarantee safety or worth.
trigger_scenario: A top-performing employee is told that promotions will no longer be based on visible metrics; panic arises because self-esteem was fused with measurable achievements.
anchor_text: Live beyond likes and dislikes, quietly present within.
